commands.review_summary:
The PR filters unresolved `${VAR}` references out of config env collection and adds focused coverage for dotenv-backed SecretRef values in managed gateway service-env planning.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +83. Total +89 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main and `v2026.6.1` shows config env values override state-dir `.env` in durable env collection, and LaunchAgent rendering only restores state-dotenv winners, so `${MINIMAX_API_KEY}` in config can mask the real dotenv value; I did not run a live managed install.

target.title: [codex] fix service env placeholder collection
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #90277.

This fixes the managed gateway service env plan when config contains an unresolved env placeholder such as `${MINIMAX_API_KEY}` and the real value lives in `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/.env`.

The change skips unresolved `${VAR}` references while collecting config env vars for runtime/service env. Concrete config env values still work, but placeholder values no longer mask the state-dir `.env` value during service env planning.

## Impact

- No config schema changes.
- No MiniMax-specific allowlist or provider special case.
- Existing concrete `config.env` values keep working.
- Env-backed SecretRefs can still resolve from the managed service environment after install/restart.
- Security behavior is slightly tighter because literal unresolved placeholders are not staged as credential-like env values.

## Verification

- `git fetch origin main`
- `git rebase origin/main`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/config.env-vars.test.ts src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts`

Not run: live macOS LaunchAgent/systemd install or restart; this PR only verifies the install-plan behavior in focused tests.
target.author: sallyom
